# Generated by openai (gpt-4)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template for a complete GCP application stack (Compute Engine, Cloud SQL, Cloud Storage, Load Balancer). Focus on core infrastructure. Skip Cloud Logging and Monitoring configuration to reduce complexity in our initial MVP deployment.
# Category: cloud_monitoring

resources:

  # Compute Engine
  - name: my-vm-instance
    type: compute.v1.instance
    properties:
      zone: us-central1-a
      machineType: zones/us-central1-a/machineTypes/n1-standard-1
      disks:
        - deviceName: boot
          type: PERSISTENT
          boot: true
          autoDelete: true
          initializeParams:
            sourceImage: projects/debian-cloud/global/images/family/debian-9
  
  # Cloud SQL
  - name: my-sql-instance
    type: sqladmin.v1beta4.instance
    properties:
      region: us-central1
      settings:
        tier: db-n1-standard-1
  
  # Cloud Storage
  - name: my-storage-bucket
    type: storage.v1.bucket
    properties:
      location: US
  
  # Load Balancer
  - name: my-frontend
    type: compute.v1.urlMap
    properties:
      defaultService: my-backend
  
  # Infrastructure - Backend
  - name: my-backend
    type: compute.v1.backendService
    properties:
      backends:
        - group: $(ref.my-instance-group.selfLink)
  
  # Infrastructure - Health Check
  - name: my-health-check
    type: compute.v1.httpHealthCheck
    properties:
      requestPath: /health-check
  
  # Infrastructure - Backend Service
  - name: my-backend-service
    type: compute.v1.targetHttpProxy
    properties:
      urlMap: $(ref.my-url-map.selfLink)
      
  # Network
  - name: my-network
    type: compute.v1.network
    properties:
      autoCreateSubnetworks: true
      
  # Sub Network
  - name: my-subnetwork
    type: compute.v1.subnetwork
    properties:
      region: us-central1
      ipCidrRange: 10.0.0.0/16
      network: $(ref.my-network.selfLink)
